lede: A look at October 7 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Niels Bohr and Vladimir Putin, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hamas
events.desc: Hamas and several other Palestinian militant groups launch an attack into Israel, which results in the deaths of around 1,200, mostly civilians, and the taking of 251 hostages, including civilians and soldiers. The attack initiated the Gaza war and the larger Middle Eastern crisis.

events.title: 2008 TC3
events.desc: Asteroid 2008 TC3 impacts the Earth over Sudan, the first time an asteroid impact is detected prior to its entry into Earth's atmosphere.

events.title: Operation Breakthrough
events.desc: A hunter discovers three gray whales trapped under the ice near Alaska; the situation becomes a multinational effort to free the whales.

events.title: 1985 Puerto Rico floods
events.desc: The Mameyes landslide kills almost 200 people in Puerto Rico.

events.title: Mother Teresa
events.desc: Mother Teresa establishes the Missionaries of Charity.

events.title: East Germany
events.desc: The communist German Democratic Republic (East Germany) is formed.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The McCollum memo proposes bringing the United States into the war in Europe by provoking the Japanese to attack the United States.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: A US Navy ship captures a Confederate raider in a Brazilian seaport.

events.title: William II of the Netherlands
events.desc: Willem II becomes King of the Netherlands.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The Americans defeat British forces under general John Burgoyne in the Second Battle of Saratoga, also known as the Battle of Bemis Heights, compelling Burgoyne's eventual surrender.

births.name: Niels Bohr
births.line: Danish physicist and philosopher, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Vladimir Putin
births.line: Russian colonel and politician, 4th President of Russia

births.name: Donald Tsang
births.line: Chinese civil servant and politician, 2nd Chief Executive of Hong Kong

births.name: Desmond Tutu
births.line: South African archbishop and activist, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Jurelang Zedkaia, Marshallese politician, 5th President of the Marshall Islands
Edgar Allan Poe, American short story writer, poet, and critic
Emperor Shizong of Liao, emperor of the Liao Dynasty

observances.text: Christian feast day: Henry Muhlenberg (some Lutheran Churches, Episcopal Church of the USA)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of the Rosary
